ingest.title: Import Books
ingest.subtitle: Upload an EPUB directly or scan a server directory to stage a batch.

direct_upload.title: Add one EPUB right now
direct_upload.description: The file goes straight into staging so you can review metadata, merge duplicates, and publish it into the catalog.

scan.title: Queue a batch from import roots
scan.description: Scanning is useful when the server already has a mounted folder of EPUBs. The backend only accepts configured import roots and this action is restricted to permitted paths.
scan.field_hint: Use an absolute server path inside `IMPORT_SCAN_ROOTS`.
scan.success_message: Books will appear in staging soon.

workflow.title: What happens next
workflow.stage_description: Every import lands in staging first so metadata can be reviewed before it becomes visible in the catalog.
workflow.resolve_description: Use merge tools and staged details to reconcile books that already exist.
workflow.publish_description: Once staged data looks right, publish it into the shared catalog for signed-in readers.

queue_cta.title: Review staged imports after each batch.
queue_cta.message: The staging area is where metadata cleanup, duplicate handling, and publishing decisions should happen.

page_subtitle: Review, edit, and promote uploaded books to your library.
empty_state: No books in the staging area.

summary.queued_description: Books waiting for promotion into the shared catalog.
summary.selection_description: Use batch actions when you already know the queue is clean.

progress.title: Scan Progress
progress.auto_refresh: New staged books will appear automatically.

staged.merge_help: Search the shared catalog for the book that should absorb this staged entry, then merge the staged metadata into that record.
staged.no_match: No staged books matched “{{author}}”. Clear the filter or upload a new batch.
staged.empty_filtered_message: Try another author name or clear the filter to see the full queue.
staged.delete_selected_message: This removes the selected items from staging. Use this when the imports are definitely not worth keeping.
staged.promote_all_message: This publishes every currently matched staged book into the shared catalog. Only do this when the queue is already clean.

editor.description: Review and normalize the staged metadata before publishing it into the shared catalog.

confirmations.delete_staged_message: This removes the staged entry and its current staging metadata. Use this when the import is bad, duplicated, or no longer needed.
confirmations.delete_staged_from_queue_message: This removes the staged entry from the queue. Use this when the import should not continue to review or publication.
